source.title: Fatal Chemical Decomposition Reaction and Explosion at Optima Belle LLC

actions_taken: Local authorities issued shelter-in-place order for region within two miles for over four hours
Chemours Belle site fire brigade responded
Post-incident chemical reaction hazard testing conducted
event.summary: On December 8, 2020, at approximately 10:00 p.m., a pressure-rated rotary double cone dryer containing sodium dichloroisocyanurate dihydrate (NaDCC dihydrate, CDB-56®) exploded at Optima Belle LLC in Belle, West Virginia. The explosion occurred during a toll manufacturing operation in which Optima Belle was dehydrating CDB-56® to produce anhydrous sodium dichloroisocyanurate (CDB-63®) on behalf of Clearon Corporation through a contractual agreement with tolling outsourcing partner Richman Chemical Inc. (RCI). The chlorinated isocyanurate compound underwent an unexpected self-accelerating decomposition reaction, releasing gases that increased the dryer internal pressure above its design pressure, causing the dryer to explode. Metal debris and dryer fragments propelled off-site and within the facility, striking a methanol pipe that subsequently caught fire. One Optima Belle employee (John Mark Gillenwater II) was fatally injured, two others were evaluated for respiratory irritation, and one Kanawha County resident reported a minor leg injury. Debris was found almost a half mile from the site. Estimated property damage was $33.1 million.
recommendations: Optima Belle: Develop and implement a written thermal and reactive hazards evaluation and management program adhering to CCPS guidance (2021-02-I-WV-R1)

Optima Belle: Develop and implement a written program for tolling process design and equipment selection using CCPS guidance to ensure equipment design basis is adequate, safeguards are properly designed, and new processes are evaluated at laboratory and pilot scales before production scale (2021-02-I-WV-R2)

Optima Belle: Develop and implement a formalized program for toll manufacturing agreements using CCPS resources to identify roles and responsibilities, evaluate equipment requirements, and ensure participation by all parties (2021-02-I-WV-R3)
Optima Belle: Develop and implement a process safety management system consistent with CCPS guidance addressing hazard identification, risk analysis, and management of risk (2021-02-I-WV-R4)

Clearon: Develop and implement a comprehensive process knowledge management program ensuring robust process technology and safety information packages, awareness of critical reactive chemistry information, and procedures for transmittal to toll manufacturers (2021-02-I-WV-R5)

Clearon: Update the sodium dichloroisocyanurate dihydrate (CDB-56®) safety data sheet to provide reasoning for storage and decomposition temperatures, explain differences between decomposition temperature and lowest temperature for self-accelerating decomposition, and provide exothermic decomposition energy (2021-02-I-WV-R6)
Clearon: Develop and implement a written program for tolling process design and equipment selection using CCPS guidance (2021-02-I-WV-R7)
Clearon: Develop and implement a formalized program for toll manufacturing agreements using CCPS resources (2021-02-I-WV-R8)
Clearon: Develop and implement a process safety management system consistent with CCPS guidance (2021-02-I-WV-R9)

RCI: Develop and implement a formalized program for toll manufacturing agreements using CCPS resources to identify roles and responsibilities and ensure participation by all parties (2021-02-I-WV-R10)
OSHA: Update the Chemical Reactivity Hazards website to include various reactivity assessment tools developed since 2002 (2021-02-I-WV-R11)
OSHA: Develop and implement a comprehensive outreach plan to ensure chemical industry awareness of the Chemical Reactivity Hazards website (2021-02-I-WV-R12)

OSHA: Amend the Process Safety Management (PSM) Standard, 29 CFR 1910.119, to achieve more comprehensive control of reactive hazards by broadening application to cover reactive hazards from process-specific conditions and combinations of chemicals, requiring consultation of multiple information sources, and augmenting process hazard analysis to explicitly require evaluation of reactive hazards (2021-02-I-WV-R13)

NCBI: Update safety information in PubChem for sodium dichloroisocyanurate (NaDCC) dihydrate to include reactivity and decomposition information including SADT, explosion hazard, and DSC and ARC results (2021-02-I-WV-R14)

CCPS: Update Guidelines for Process Safety in Outsourced Manufacturing Operations or develop new tolling guidance document to include current best practices, guidance specific to tolling brokers, and alignment with comprehensive management systems framework (2021-02-I-WV-R15)

hazards.name: Thermal decomposition of sodium dichloroisocyanurate dihydrate (NaDCC dihydrate)
Inadequate equipment design for reactive chemical processing

threats.name: Heating of NaDCC dihydrate above self-accelerating decomposition temperature (SADT)
Uncontrolled runaway decomposition reaction in closed vessel
Inadequate understanding of thermal stability and decomposition hazards
Lack of effective process knowledge management and communication
Use of production-scale equipment without laboratory or pilot-scale testing
Inadequate thermal hazard assessment and reactive hazard screening
Insufficient pressure relief design for decomposition gases
Inadequate cooling capability during exothermic reaction

consequences.name: Release of toxic chlorine gas and nitrogen trichloride
Injuries to workers and community members

controls.name: Process Knowledge Management System
controls.barrier_role: Ensure comprehensive understanding and documentation of chemical hazards and decomposition risks

supporting_text: Clearon lacked effective process knowledge management systems, and as a result the Technology Package that Clearon delivered to Optima Belle as part of the tolling arrangement did not adequately communicate the circumstances and temperatures that could lead to the hazardous decomposition of CDB-56®
inadequate literature searches for publicly available or internal Clearon-owned NaDCC dihydrate data
The CSB does not have evidence that a comprehensive evaluation was conducted to identify the potential hazards of using an alternate dryer

controls.name: Thermal Hazard Assessment
controls.barrier_role: Identify and evaluate thermal stability and decomposition hazards of NaDCC dihydrate
controls.lod_basis: Industry best practice (CCPS guidance, ASTM standards)

supporting_text: None of the parties involved in the tolling operation effectively assessed the hazards of the NaDCC dihydrate or operation
failure to identify the initiation of a NaDCC dihydrate decomposition as a credible scenario, except as a result of a decomposition temperature greater than 240°C

During the incident, a runaway decomposition reaction had begun in the dryer by the time the dryer temperature reached 83°C—a temperature significantly lower than the identified decomposition temperature of 240°C
failure to utilize available reactive hazard screening methods to assess the potential reactivity or explosivity of heated NaDCC dihydrate inside a metal pressure vessel

controls.name: Equipment Design and Selection for Reactive Chemical Processing
controls.barrier_role: Ensure equipment is properly designed, sized, and engineered for safe handling of reactive chemicals with adequate cooling and pressure relief
controls.lod_basis: Industry best practice (CCPS guidance, ASME standards)

supporting_text: Optima Belle used its existing production equipment for the CDB-56® dehydration, but this equipment was not designed, sized, or re-engineered for CDB-56® dehydration
the equipment was not designed to quickly cool the dryer contents or to relieve the excess pressure generated during the decomposition reaction

Clearon and Optima Belle essentially conducted an experiment on a new method to remove water of hydration from the CDB-56® at full production scale (involving over 8,000 pounds) without first experimenting at the laboratory and pilot scales

controls.name: Scaled Testing Program (Laboratory and Pilot Scale)
controls.barrier_role: Evaluate process hazards and gain thermal stability data before production-scale operation

supporting_text: Had scaled studies been conducted, Optima Belle, Clearon, and RCI likely would have gained additional NaDCC dihydrate thermal stability data, reactivity information, and process knowledge before running the first production-scale batch, which might have led to changes in the process and potentially prevented the decomposition reaction and the explosion

controls.name: Toll Manufacturing Agreement and Governance
controls.barrier_role: Establish clear roles, responsibilities, and hazard assessment procedures for all parties in tolling arrangement
controls.lod_basis: Industry best practice (CCPS Guidelines for Process Safety in Outsourced Manufacturing Operations)

supporting_text: The dryer explosion might have been prevented had Clearon and Optima Belle applied the suggested industry guidance

The 'Process' Section of the Technology Package provided to RCI and Optima Belle states: 'Toll manufacturer must develop the specific process for manufacturing at their facility. Clearon will review the process developed by the toll manufacturer to confirm it meets the requirements of this technology package'

RCI asked Clearon if an alternate drying technology—such as a rotary double cone dryer—was acceptable. Clearon responded, saying it 'could be viable' and 'maybe worth a conversation.' The CSB does not have evidence that a comprehensive evaluation was conducted

controls.name: Safety Data Sheet (SDS) Accuracy and Completeness
controls.barrier_role: Communicate accurate decomposition temperature and thermal hazard information to users

supporting_text: At the time of the incident, Clearon's NaDCC dihydrate (CDB-56®) safety data sheet (SDS) listed a NaDCC decomposition temperature of 240°C to 250°C
Post-incident testing results found that NaDCC dihydrate begins a runaway exotherm at approximately 81℃ when heated in a closed container
In the 1990s, further industry studies estimated a self-accelerating decomposition temperature (SADT) for NaDCC dihydrate between 45°C to 65°C

controls.name: Process Hazard Analysis (PHA)
controls.barrier_role: Systematically identify and evaluate hazards of the dehydration process
controls.lod_basis: Industry best practice (CCPS guidance, OSHA PSM)

supporting_text: Reactivity Evaluation Gaps by Clearon, Optima Belle, and RCI
failure to identify the initiation of a NaDCC dihydrate decomposition as a credible scenario

controls.name: Pressure Relief System Design
controls.barrier_role: Relieve excess pressure generated during decomposition reaction
controls.lod_basis: ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code

supporting_text: the equipment was not designed to quickly cool the dryer contents or to relieve the excess pressure generated during the decomposition reaction
Safety Relief Device(s) Overpressure Protection

controls.name: Cooling System Design and Capacity
controls.barrier_role: Rapidly cool dryer contents to arrest exothermic decomposition reaction
controls.lod_basis: Industry best practice for reactive chemical processing

supporting_text: the equipment was not designed to quickly cool the dryer contents or to relieve the excess pressure generated during the decomposition reaction
Jacket Cooling and Heating Design

controls.name: Temperature Monitoring and Alarm System
controls.barrier_role: Detect abnormal temperature rise and alert operators to decomposition initiation
controls.lod_basis: Industry best practice for reactive chemical processing

supporting_text: Process Monitoring and Troubleshooting

controls.name: Regulatory Coverage of Reactive Hazards (PSM/RMP)
controls.barrier_role: Mandate comprehensive process safety management for reactive chemicals
controls.lod_basis: OSHA PSM Standard (29 CFR 1910.119), EPA RMP Rule (40 CFR 68)

supporting_text: NaDCC dihydrate is not covered by either the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) Process Safety Management (PSM) standard or the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Risk Management Program (RMP) rule
Had NaDCC dihydrate been covered under the PSM standard or RMP rule, Optima Belle would have been required to implement risk mitigation and management systems that could have prevented this incident

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
